content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 17, 1988


Jennie Tullo
Legislative Assistant
PRECOR
P.O. Box 6452
Westlake Village, CA  91359

Re:  Our File No. G-88-317

Dear Ms. Tullo:

     We have received your letter concerning the status of the
proposed regulation concerning payments for lobbying services
(2 California Code of Regulations Section 18614). On June 8,
1988, the Commission approved Regulation 18614 for adoption.
We anticipate the regulation will become effective in late
September.

     Enclosed is a copy of the regulation adopted by the
Commission. To provide the background information you
requested, I also have enclosed a copy of the notice and
statement of reasons for the regulation, as well as a copy of
the staff memorandum presented to the Commission at the June 8
meeting.

     Questions about lobbying firm registration and reporting
may be addressed to our Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

KED:plh

Enclosures


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Post Office Box 6452
Westlake Village, CA 91359
(818) 889-5840

Aug 15 1 Sl [illegible] 38

10 August 1988

Kathryn Donovan
Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Dear Ms. Donovan,

Our company is currently registering as a lobbying firm. Inside the Lobbyist Package that we received, there was a Lobbying Newsletter that made reference to the Proposed Regulation 18614--Lobbying Services. The date of the Commission's meeting was April 5, 1988. We would like to know the result of that meeting, and the Proposed Regulation's implications to a lobbying firm-if possible. Any documentation of the Regulation would be helpful.

We appreciate your time on this matter. Our company is moving to Sacramento at the end of this month. We are an environmental consulting firm and are very anxious to move to the capital and get started with our new opportunities. Becoming a lobbying firm is a new field for us, and we are thankful for your assistance.

Sincerely yours,

[signature]

Jennie Tullo
Legislative Assistant

PRECOR
